summary: Split the first 0xAC bytes of the previous `melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c` text range into a new matching source file `melee/sfx/sfx_unk.c`, then marked both `sfx_unk.c` and `crowdsfx.c` as Matching in `configure.py`. The PR also moved `mpLib_80458868` from a private static anonymous struct declaration into a named `mpCollisionBox` type in `mp/types.h`, exposed it via `mp/mplib.h`, and updated `crowdsfx.c` to use typed collision-box access instead of raw `char*` pointer arithmetic.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/sfx/sfx_unk.c` containing `un_80321900` and `un_80321950`, which were previously at the start of `crowdsfx.c`.

Adjusted `config/GALE01/splits.txt` so `melee/sfx/sfx_unk.c` owns `.text` `0x80321900..0x803219AC` and `.sdata2` `0x804DE148..0x804DE14C`, while `melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c` now starts at `.text` `0x803219AC` and `.sdata2` `0x804DE150`.
Changed `configure.py` from a single `Object(NonMatching, "melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c")` to two matching objects: `melee/sfx/sfx_unk.c` and `melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c`.
Converted `symbols.txt` entries at `0x804DE148`, `0x804DE150`, and `0x804DE158` from global `un_...` symbols to local float/double labels `@175`, `@395`, and `@397`.
Moved `un_804A2F08` and `un_804D7050` definitions in `crowdsfx.c` to address-commented globals and exposed them in `crowdsfx.h` so the new split file can initialize the crowd SFX state.
Removed a raw `extern char mpLib_80458868[]` from `crowdsfx.c` and replaced the access with `mpCollisionBox* box = mpLib_80458868;` plus `box[1].bottom`.
Introduced `struct mpCollisionBox` in `src/melee/mp/types.h` with `top`, `bottom`, `left`, and `right` float fields.
Removed the anonymous static definition of `mpLib_80458868[2]` from `mplib.static.h`, defined `mpCollisionBox mpLib_80458868[2]` in `mplib.c`, and declared it extern in `mplib.h`.
Changed `mplib.h` to include `mp/types.h` instead of only `mp/forward.h`, because the public declaration of `mpLib_80458868` requires the complete `mpCollisionBox` type.
Added a forward typedef for `mpCollisionBox` in `mp/forward.h` and reordered the forward declarations.
smart_moves: The split was made at a clean function boundary: `sfx_unk.c` takes `un_80321900` and `un_80321950`, while `crowdsfx.c` begins at `fn_803219AC`, matching the updated text ranges.
The build configuration and split metadata were updated together, preventing the new file split from becoming only a source-level refactor without linker awareness.

Replacing `char*` pointer arithmetic with `mpCollisionBox` made the `crowdsfx.c` dependency on map collision bounds explicit and documented the offset formerly used as `*(f32*) (mpLib + 0x14)` as `box[1].bottom`.

Promoting `mpLib_80458868` from a static anonymous struct in `mplib.static.h` to a typed external object allowed another linked translation unit to reference the data without relying on an untyped extern.
The new split file includes `crowdsfx.h` and uses exported globals/functions rather than duplicating struct definitions or local declarations.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a matching file, update `splits.txt`, `configure.py`, and any symbol ownership changes in the same PR so the linker layout and build graph stay consistent.
A file split may force previously file-local or static data assumptions to be revisited; here `mpLib_80458868` had to become a real typed extern because `crowdsfx.c` needed it.
Raw address-based or byte-pointer access can often be eliminated by naming the underlying data structure, which improves type safety while preserving matching if field order and size are correct.
If a global is shared by a newly split translation unit, move the declaration to the relevant public header and keep the single definition in one C file.
Local constants in `.sdata2` should be represented as local labels in `symbols.txt` when they are not real global objects; this PR changed several `un_804DE...` globals into `@...` locals.
naming_conventions: Unknown SFX split file was named `sfx_unk.c`, reflecting that the extracted functions are still unidentified but belong in the sound-effects area.
New map collision bounds type was named `mpCollisionBox`, matching the existing `mp` prefix and describing the four float fields `top`, `bottom`, `left`, and `right`.
Address comments such as `/* 4A2F08 */`, `/* 4D7050 */`, and `/* 458868 */` were used on global definitions/declarations to preserve symbol-address traceability.
Unidentified functions and globals retained existing placeholder names such as `un_80321900`, `un_80321950`, `un_804A2F08`, and `un_804D7050` rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split `crowdsfx.c` into two matching objects by carving out `.text` `0x80321900..0x803219AC` into `sfx_unk.c` and shifting `crowdsfx.c` start to `0x803219AC`.
Separated `.sdata2` ownership so `sfx_unk.c` owns the float at `0x804DE148`, while `crowdsfx.c` owns later constants beginning at `0x804DE150`.
Changed build status from `NonMatching` to `Matching` for the SFX objects after the split, indicating the file boundary/data ownership change resolved the previous mismatch.

Preserved the semantic memory access formerly expressed as offset `0x14` into `mpLib_80458868` by using `box[1].bottom`, since each `mpCollisionBox` has four floats and `box[1].bottom` corresponds to base plus `0x10 + 0x4`.
Moved the data declaration for `mpLib_80458868` out of a static header into `mplib.c` to avoid incorrect per-translation-unit static storage while exposing the actual linked object.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `sfx_unk.c` functions `un_80321900` or `un_80321950`.
Find PRs that further type or rename `CrowdSFX_UnkStruct` and `gCrowdConfig`.
Find PRs touching `mpLib_80458868` or replacing more raw map collision-box accesses with `mpCollisionBox`.
Find other split-and-link PRs where a static header object had to become a typed extern.
